news.t: These commodity ETFs can help hedge against geopolitical tensions, market analyst says

overview.description: The GraniteShares Bloomberg Commodity Broad Strategy No K-1 ETF (COMB) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in broad market commodities. The fund seeks to outperform a broad commodity market index through active management of the funds collateral. The index include futures contracts on up to 24 different commodities. COMB was launched on May 22, 2017 and is issued by GraniteShares.

data.title: China's reopening sees an incremental rebound in consumer spending, says Kraneshares' Brendan Ahern
data.text: CNBC's Seema Mody joins Kraneshares' Brendan Ahern and Granite Shares' Will Rhind for a 'Halftime Report' ETF Edge to discuss China's Q2 earnings numbers, investor perspectives on China's reopening, a...

data.title: These commodity ETFs can help hedge against geopolitical tensions, market analyst says
data.text: Exchange-traded funds invested in oil and gold can help buffer portfolios against rising geopolitical risks, Astoria Advisors CEO John Davi says.

data.title: Commodities make a comeback. How one ETF is navigating the group
data.text: GraniteShares founder and CEO Will Rhind and George Milling-Stanley, chief gold strategist at State Street's SPDR ETFs, share their takes on commodities' performance in 2021 with CNBC's Bob Pisani.

data.title: Silver ETFs spike. Digging into whether a short squeeze could work
data.text: William Rhind, the founder and CEO of GraniteShares, talks the sharp moves higher in silver and silver ETFs and whether a short squeeze could work with CNBC's Bob Pisani.

data.title: ETF Mythbuster: Do ETFs cause volatility?
data.text: We will debunk the common misconception on Wall Street and give a model portfolio to tackle volatility for the new year .
